summary: Matched the fighter translation unit src/melee/ft/ft_0877.c and removed its 804-line assembly fallback. The build metadata was flipped from NonMatching/asm to Matching/C in configure.py and obj_files.mk. The C-side edits were small but targeted: they adjusted ft_80087D0C's argument naming and temporary variable flow, rewrote a Popo/Nana case-13 check as an explicit fighter-kind switch with inclusive SFX range tests, moved/reshaped stack padding, and did minor formatting in ft_80087988. There were no PR body comments or review comments in the slice, so intent beyond matching is inferred from the diff.

key_files.role: deleted assembly fallback after C matched
matched fighter C implementation with small logic and stack-shape edits
changed melee/ft/ft_0877.c from NonMatching to Matching
swapped object from asm/melee/ft/ft_0877.s.o to src/melee/ft/ft_0877.c.o

what_changed: Deleted asm/melee/ft/ft_0877.s, an 804-line assembly file containing ft_800877F8 through ft_80088148.
Marked melee/ft/ft_0877.c as Object(Matching, ...) in configure.py instead of Object(NonMatching, ...).
Updated obj_files.mk so the text object list builds src/melee/ft/ft_0877.c.o instead of asm/melee/ft/ft_0877.s.o.
In ft_80087988, kept the same parasol-status test for statuses 4 and 5 but formatted the OR condition on one line.
Renamed ft_80087D0C's second parameter from arg1 to sfx_id and passed sfx_id to lbAudioAx_800233EC.
Removed a local u8 _[12] padding declaration near the start of ft_80087D0C and introduced int _[1] later in the case block around lbAudioAx_800230C8 outputs.
In ft_80087D0C case 0, for selected SFX IDs, routed ft_80087C70's result through sfx_id, conditionally added 3 when fighter->x2223_b7 is set, then assigned back to sfx.
In ft_80087D0C case 13, replaced a FighterKind temporary and unusual range comparison with an explicit switch over fighter->kind for FTKIND_POPO and FTKIND_NANA.
Changed the case-13 SFX range checks from strict inequalities to inclusive bounds: 0x1FBFD <= sfx <= 0x1FC62 and 0x1FC63 <= sfx <= 0x1FCC8.
Preserved the audio adjustment behavior using ftCommon_80080144: add 0x66 for one Popo/Nana SFX range when true, subtract 0x66 for the paired range when not true.
smart_moves: The final match was achieved without broad rewrites: only 14 added and 15 deleted C lines were needed to replace 804 lines of assembly.
Using an explicit switch on fighter->kind for FTKIND_POPO and FTKIND_NANA likely made the source closer to the compiler's emitted control flow than the prior compound comparison.
Separating sfx_id from sfx in ft_80087D0C gave the compiler a variable lifetime/register-allocation shape that matched the assembly after ft_80087C70 and the x2223_b7 adjustment.
The small dummy stack object was relocated and typed as int _[1], suggesting the match depended on stack layout near the lbAudioAx_800230C8 call rather than on a generic early u8 padding array.
The PR performed the complete decomp cleanup: delete asm, switch configure.py to Matching, and update obj_files.mk to build the C object.
decomp_lessons: When finishing a file match, verify both build systems: configure.py's object classification and obj_files.mk's source-vs-asm object path must agree.
For matching, semantically equivalent C comparisons can produce different branch structures; an explicit enum switch can match better than a hand-written compound condition.
Inclusive versus exclusive bounds matter for both correctness and matching when reconstructing compiler-generated range checks.
Temporary variable naming is not only readability: splitting or reusing variables can influence lifetimes and register allocation enough to match.
Stack padding should be placed where the compiler needs the frame shape; moving from a broad early u8 buffer to a narrow local int array can be a useful tactic.
After deleting an asm fallback, keep searchable evidence of the removed symbol range because future regressions may need to compare old assembly for functions like ft_80087D0C and ft_80088148.
naming_conventions: The file is named ft_0877.c / ft_0877.s even though the PR title says ft_8077; the head branch and changed paths consistently use ft_0877.
Unidentified fighter functions remain address-style names such as ft_80087D0C and ft_80087C70.
The second argument of ft_80087D0C was clarified from generic arg1 to sfx_id, matching its role as an audio SFX identifier.
Existing fighter enum names FTKIND_POPO and FTKIND_NANA were used instead of numeric fighter-kind comparisons.
Bitfield-style fighter fields such as fighter->x2223_b7 remained in use where no semantic name was introduced.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the assembly file only after the C object was declared Matching and selected in obj_files.mk.

Control-flow matching in ft_80087D0C used an explicit switch on fighter->kind and a break inside case 13 while allowing the surrounding switch's fallthrough into cases 6 through 33 to remain as in the source.
Variable lifetime matching used sfx_id as an intermediate result for ft_80087C70, then copied it back into sfx after the optional +3 adjustment.
Stack-frame matching used a small unused local int _[1] near the audio-range-output locals instead of an earlier u8 _[12].
The Popo/Nana audio remap used exact inclusive constants 0x1FBFD, 0x1FC62, 0x1FC63, and 0x1FCC8, plus +/-0x66 adjustments.
review_feedback: No PR body, issue comments, review comments, or review approvals were present in the provided slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ft_80087D0C or document its audio SFX remapping behavior.
Search for other matching PRs that use dummy local arrays like int _[1] for stack-frame matching.
Search for Popo/Nana or Ice Climbers audio remap code around FTKIND_POPO, FTKIND_NANA, and ftCommon_80080144.
Check whether the PR title typo ft_8077 was repeated elsewhere or if all actual files are ft_0877.
